text: Being here, I feel a combination of curiosity and disquiet. The glass wall between me and the brightly lit display room is both inviting and isolating, like the threshold of new ideas waiting to be crossed. The room promises engagement with questions of perception and intention, but I hesitate, aware of my own awkwardness among all this innovation. I wonder about the boundaries we construct—in architecture, in thought, in social encounter. Am I participant or observer? The prospect of discovery stirs in me both excitement and self-doubt.
